> right, does not support that, 1) and 2) have different meanings

There is another patch http://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/16359/, it does similar things as your patch.

I think that we can use the new option as that "--replace-mgsnode=xxx", the "xxx" is the new mgsnode what will overwrite the exist mgsnode parameter. If "xxx" is empty, means remove mgsnode parameter.

parents.subject: LU-4781 test: wait for dbench to stop

commit.subject: LU-6960 utils: erase mgsnode param with tunefs.lustre
commit.message:
LU-6960 utils: erase mgsnode param with tunefs.lustre

When we need to change MGS node, "tunefs.lustre --mgsnode" would only
append to existing mgsnode params.

This patch add a "--erase-mgsnode" parameter to wipe existing mgsnode
and append mgsnode parameters afterwards.

Signed-off-by: Bobi Jam <bobijam.xu@intel.com>
Change-Id: I90799a0338e9f4a5f2a606388ea3dd0384ae703f
